title: acaena inermis
intro: Acaena inermis, also known as the woolly cat's tail or woolly button, is a perennial plant species native to New Zealand, characterized by its woolly, white foliage and yellow flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Acaena inermis include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. The plant is often prepared as an infusion or tincture for medicinal use, although its efficacy and safety have not been extensively scientifically studied.
